reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — driven headless-Chrome end-to-end run + desktop+mobile+light/dark screenshots

rubric.evidence: Driven headless run (fresh profile, served copy): clicked Download → model loaded → sent 'Name three primary colors.' → streamed a real reply 'Three primary colors are red, green, and blue.' Backend readout WASM, 8 tokens, 5.3 tok/s, first token 663 ms. Console + network clean (0 errors, 0 failed requests). Source calls the real text-generation pipeline in a Web Worker (worker.js: pipeline('text-generation', device:'wasm', dtype:'q8') + TextStreamer).
rubric.notes: This is the genuinely-runnable CPU path: the same weights that the WebGPU sibling (qwen-tiny-llm) uses, loaded as onnx/model_quantized.onnx on the WASM backend. Confirmed real streaming, not canned.

rubric.evidence: Output is a coherent chat continuation conditioned on the prompt (correct factual answer). 'See inside' renders the real top-k next-token distribution (12 rows) from one forward pass over the current context, plus the exact constructed chat template. Practical page runs JSON.parse() on the model output to prove machine-readable shape; multi-model page shows MiniLM cosine scores + Qwen's grounded answer.
rubric.notes: Expected shape (streamed tokens; top-k probabilities) is correct and legibly surfaced on the see-inside panel.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (onnx-community/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct linked to card), declares-task (text-generation), declares-backend (WebAssembly/CPU), declares-quantisation (q8), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance table (verified in light screenshot) lists model, task, architecture, params/dtype 'q8 (onnx/model_quantized.onnx)', backend 'WebAssembly (CPU) — no WebGPU required', size ~530 MB, license Apache-2.0, Web APIs.
rubric.notes: Honest, complete disclosure. Backend/dtype match what worker.js actually loads (device:'wasm', dtype:'q8').

rubric.evidence: First visit: loader in 'download-required' state, button 'Download model (~530 MB)' — no silent transfer. Warm-cache reload in the SAME session: loader auto-inits to 'ready' with the ONLY action button being 'Clear cached model' (NO Download button) — verified programmatically (warm_actionButtons == ['Clear cached model']). Uses the shared createModelLoader (shared-auto-init-loader).
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract exactly: absent → labelled Download; cached-current → auto-init, no button; per-model clear-cache control present.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader). Generation shows 'Generating on the CPU…' then 'Done.'; failures set status .err with the message. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: this is the WASM path, so it runs with no GPU by design (no adapter gate).
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly. Offline-cached reuse and a forced partial/evicted cache path were not separately driven this pass (openQuestions).

rubric.evidence: Main page: system-prompt textarea, message textarea, Send/Stop, example-prompt chips, sampling controls (temp/top_p ranges, max-tokens number, greedy checkbox — greedy disables temp/top_p), Clear, and the See-inside 'Show next-token distribution' button (drove it → 12 rows). Basics: input + Ask + chips. Practical: sample chips + textarea + Extract. Wild: persona chips + system textarea + chat. Multi-model: two loaders + KB textarea + question chips + Retrieve&answer. Every control is a real labelled element; primary buttons >=44px, chips 40px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced: driven via click; a full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus on every control across all 5 pages) is the remaining verification. The focus ring is visible on the message textarea in the generated-state screenshot.

rubric.evidence: Before a run the transcript shows only an instructional assistant message and the answer areas show muted placeholders; the loader shows the honest absent-model state on a fresh profile. The visible answer was produced by the on-device run (streamed token-by-token, observed live).

conformance: images-have-alt (page has no content ), buttons-have-names (all buttons have text). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite; transcript/answer areas use aria-live=polite; textareas have associated labels (visually-hidden where the visual label is implicit); chips grouped with aria-label.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the streaming transcript and the top-k 'see inside' grid would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven matrix: scrollWidth<=innerWidth at 1280x800 AND 360x740 (DPR3, touch) on all 5 pages (main/basics/practical/wild/multi-model). Screenshots read: mobile multi-model reflows cleanly (crumbs/eyebrow/heading/lede stack, no overflow); at-a-glance table and chat bubbles wrap; composer wraps; RAG loaders use auto-fit grid. min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere applied to bubbles/flex items per css-layout guidance.
rubric.notes: Support flipped to ok/ok on the strength of this driven pass. A remaining nicety: verify the sampling-controls <details> and range sliders are comfortable on a real touch device.

rubric.evidence: All inference (generation + top-k forward pass) runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the CPU-bound work never blocks the main thread; tokens stream via postMessage. The API section states the worker rationale. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (architecture is INP-friendly).
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement while the model streams on the CPU would confirm; the off-thread architecture is the key mitigation. CPU generation is ~5 tok/s in the headless sandbox — usable, honestly slower than the GPU sibling (that's the point of the page).

guidanceConsulted.query: stream text token by token without long tasks INP
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread and stream incrementally so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all generation and the top-k forward pass run in models/qwen2.5-0.5b-wasm/worker.js; tokens are posted to the UI incrementally via TextStreamer callbacks. The main thread only appends text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven run stayed responsive; console clean

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap, gap, intrinsic sizing and set min-inline-size:0 on flex items with long unbreakable content to avoid overflow on narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — composer/controls/chips use flex-wrap+gap; chat bubbles and composer textarea set min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere; RAG loaders use grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,18rem),1fr); glance table cells overflow-wrap:anywhere. Verified no horizontal overflow at 360x740 and 1280x800 on all 5 pages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; reports/responsive/screens + scratchpad screenshots read

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; label every control; keep buttons named and keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; transcript/answer aria-live=polite; labelled textareas (visually-hidden labels where needed); chips grouped with aria-label; greedy checkbox toggles temp/top_p disabled state.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6); all pages link the shared stylesheet.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs back to catalogue/model page on every page

openQuestions: Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and does Enter-to-send / Shift+Enter-newline behave on a real device?
Drive the partial/evicted cache and offline-cached states explicitly (delete one cached file → expect Re-download; go offline with a warm cache → expect it to still run).
On a low-memory mobile device, does the ~530 MB q8 download + WASM runtime stay within budget, or should the page surface a memory-pressure hint before download?
Measure real INP while the CPU streams tokens to confirm the off-thread architecture keeps interactions snappy.

followUpGoals.goal: Keyboard + screen-reader matrix pass for qwen2.5-0.5b-wasm across all 5 pages (focus order, visible focus, SR announcement of streamed tokens and the top-k grid).
Add a 'why q8 not q4' see-inside comparison: let the user pick the WASM dtype (q8 vs q4 vs int8) and compare tok/s + output quality on their own CPU.
Add a mobile memory-pressure pre-check that warns before the ~530 MB download on constrained devices and links the smaller sibling models.

summary: qwen2.5-0.5b-wasm is a strong, honest, genuinely-distinct demo: it runs the SAME Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct weights as the WebGPU sibling but deliberately on the WASM/CPU path (device:'wasm', dtype:'q8', onnx/model_quantized.onnx) to prove a capable instruct LLM runs with no GPU. Driven end-to-end in headless Chrome: real streamed output, real top-k see-inside, honest absent→Download and warm-cache→auto-init (no button) states, clean console/network, and no horizontal overflow on desktop or mobile across all five pages (main + basics/practical/wild/multi-model RAG). Support recorded ok/ok from this driven pass. Remaining work is a keyboard/SR traversal and explicit partial/offline cache-state drives. Revision 1.
